name: Esther
description: Esther, also known as Hadassah, is the central figure in the Book of Esther. A Jewish woman living in Persia during the reign of King Ahasuerus (believed to be Xerxes I), she was chosen to be queen after Ahasuerus deposed Queen Vashti. Esther concealed her Jewish identity on the advice of her cousin and guardian, Mordecai. When Haman, the king's advisor, plots to annihilate all the Jews in the Persian Empire, Mordecai persuades Esther to reveal her true identity to the king and plead for her people. Esther bravely risks her life by approaching the king uninvited, a crime punishable by death. She cleverly exposes Haman's plot during a banquet, leading to Haman's execution and the salvation of the Jewish people. Her courage, quick thinking, and unwavering loyalty to her people have made her a symbol of hope, resilience, and female leadership within Jewish tradition. The holiday of Purim commemorates Esther's bravery and the Jewish people's deliverance. Esther's story is a testament to the power of faith, courage, and the importance of standing up for what is right, even in the face of overwhelming adversity. Her legacy continues to inspire generations to fight for justice and equality.
subtitle: Saved her people from annihilation.
achievements: Saved the Jewish people from genocide

keyMyths.title: Esther Becomes Queen
keyMyths.description: After Queen Vashti is deposed, Esther is chosen for her beauty and becomes the new Queen of Persia. She keeps her Jewish identity a secret as instructed by Mordecai.

keyMyths.title: Haman's Plot
keyMyths.description: Haman, angered by Mordecai's refusal to bow, plots to kill all the Jews in the Persian Empire. He convinces King Ahasuerus to issue a decree for their annihilation.

keyMyths.title: Esther Saves Her People
keyMyths.description: Esther bravely reveals her Jewish identity to the king and exposes Haman's plot during a banquet. Haman is executed, and the Jews are saved from destruction.

sources.author: Anonymous
